extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint attacked at 5km with Froome watching, exploiting Quintana/Nibali's fixation on Froome to take yellow.
tactical_patterns: Sky deployed dual-leader decoy: Thomas attacked while rivals watched Froome, then Froome counter-attacked to split pursuers' attention.
Dumoulin surprised by committing on downhill alone, but lacked support—revealing his willingness to isolate himself early.
Nibali/Bahrain rode tempo on penultimate climb helping Sky close gaps, bizarre strategic gift to strongest team.
Movistar's three-card attack (Landa/Quintana/Valverde) came "flat-footed" late, suggesting poor coordination when initial surge failed.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: attacked before Froome contrary to expected Sky hierarchy, showed Dauphine form carried through
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: counter-attacks splintered group after Thomas went, maintained closeness at 1:25 for tomorrow's battle
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: tactical surprise descending alone, rare willingness to isolate on technical terrain
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: set tempo helping Sky then attacked only once, mysterious passivity suggests off-form or confused strategy
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: highest Movistar finisher at 2:56 after hard crash Sunday, climbed better than Quintana
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: fixated on Froome's wheel, couldn't respond when Froome accelerated, wrong climb profile excuse signals vulnerability
